title: GitHub Profile, Repository Health, and Verifiable Work · Career Evidence Triage
summary: A realistic github profile, repository health, and verifiable work artifact contains one plausible targeting, evidence, integrity, access, privacy, process, or decision defect. Locate the first unsupported claim before revising it. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in selective GitHub evidence route and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.
Write a concise profile route that helps a reviewer reach role, proof, boundaries, contact choice, and accessible alternatives.
Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Project Selection
steps.instruction:
debug case — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The debug variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.

Inspect the Project Selection trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Project Selection.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Project Selection, keep Project Selection connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Project Selection, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Project Selection, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Project Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Project Selection, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The debug variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Project Selection, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: More pinned repositories always create a stronger profile.

stimulus.title: Project Selection case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The debug variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: More pinned repositories always create a stronger profile.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure. Artifact under review: selective GitHub evidence route.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Project Selection, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Project Selection.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Reproducible Proof
steps.instruction:
Append a career-reproducible-proof-xsfgdv: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Reproducible Proof.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Reproducible Proof into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Reproducible Proof, keep Reproducible Proof connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
career-reproducible-proof-xsfgdv:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Reproducible Proof, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Reproducible Proof precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The debug variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Reproducible Proof, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A passing historical workflow badge proves the project works now.
